avoid.reason: The market is highly liquid and tightly priced, but the supplied data contain no current inflation, labor-market, or FOMC projections sufficient to establish an independent edge of at least 8%.

No qualifying edge. This contract begins on February 26, so it is not an exact complement to the zero-cuts contract, and no current authoritative macroeconomic release was supplied to support a materially different forecast.

avoid.market: Two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth in Q4 2026 through Q4 2027
avoid.reason: The long forecast horizon and nonstandard recession definition create substantial uncertainty. Without current GDP, labor, credit, or leading-indicator data, a credible 8% edge cannot be established.

notes: The market surface indicates an inflationary regime: roughly 81% pricing for zero Fed cuts, July year-over-year CPI concentrated near 3.3%-3.5%, and elevated commodity strike levels. Only three contracts passed the required volume and spread filters. None had sufficient supplied real-world grounding to justify a threshold-sized trade.
